Name: EEG recordings during sham neurofeedback in virtual reality
Authors: Collaborators from the Experimental and Computational Neuroscience Group
Acknowledgements: We thank all participants for their contribution to this study and the research group for technical support.
HowToAcknowledge: Please cite the associated manuscript and the MNE-BIDS toolbox (Appelhoff et al., 2019) when using these data.
Funding: This work was supported by CONICET (Argentina) and Universidad Nacional de Entre Ríos.

GeneratedBy.Name: MNE-BIDS
GeneratedBy.Description: Used for conversion of .cnt EEG recordings to BIDS format.

SourceDatasets.Description: Original raw EEG data in .cnt format.

EthicsApprovals: Approved by the Ethics Committee of Entre Ríos Province, Argentina.
InstitutionAddress: Ruta Provincial 11, Km 10,5, Oro Verde, Entre Ríos, Argentina
InstitutionalDepartmentName: Facultad de Ingeniería - Experimental and Computational Neuroscience Group
SoftwareFilters: High-pass filter 0.1 Hz, low-pass filter 500 Hz, notch 50 Hz
TaskDescription: Participants received sham neurofeedback (positive, negative, or no feedback) in a VR environment while EEG was recorded. The task aimed to assess the influence of feedback valence on alpha-band modulation during an attentional task. During the resting state task, two conditions were performed: eyes open (fixation cross) and eyes closed (1.5 min each conditon).
Instructions: Participants were instructed to remain relaxed, minimize movements, and pay attention to the sound of an object in the VR environment
StimulusPresentation: Visual and auditory stimuli were presented using a VR headset developed in Unity3D, synchronized with event markers sent to the EEG system.
